Carl Clark, More Opinions And The Chiefs

Carl Clark was in the Navy and travelled extensively. That introduction to the world has netted him many friends and benefits. In fact, he earned the nickname “Sweet Pea” because he is generally a likeable guy.

Because of that, he is well known across the metropolitan area, especially in social circles. He is the “grand-master of the 6902 Club” and has served as president of the prestigious Ivanhoe Club.

But, his real passion is the safety of children. You can see him any day at Fifty First and Main, caring for the children who have to cross the street to Visitation Catholic school. For doing such a good job and being well-liked, he is known as the “priest of safety”. Since 1995, he has been there on that corner, doing what he feels is his calling....protecting the kids from predators and crazy drivers.

But, Carl is best known for his role in the Monday Niters, He is so opinionated that his fellow friends are noting that he is getting more like that Ike Gardner, everyday.

Carl, who used to support the Chiefs is now voting against them, week after week. In fact, he said the Chiefs will not win more than five games this season. To his chagrin,the Chiefs have already won four and are looking any day for that fifth victory. .So at his house Monday night, everyone was kidding Carl about the above picture....that shows him holding up the number “five”.

Like other doubters, Carl is having to rethink his early predictions and yet he holds to his prediction and says the Chiefs will lose to Tennessee Titans by a field goal..

Except for Ike, his fellow Monday Niters disagree. Let’s now look in and see what the rest of the guys think.

Dr. Charles Allen: Doc is still concerned about this Alzheimer’s disease. Particularly, he is warning all care-givers that it takes more than “24-7” to look after those, with Alzheimer’s. But he is quite optimistic about the Chiefs, especially the coaching aspect. Thus, he sees Sunday as another victory for Kansas City. Chiefs by 7.

PAUL BOOKER: Interested in all the violence, Booker has high praise for Jackson County. The County has just released a plan to curtail some of the violence and he is elated about that. And he’s happy about the Chiefs being 4-0. He says it is strictly because of those linebackers....Hali, Houston and Johnson. Chiefs by 10

Bubba Cleaver: He is up to his neck in dealing with the government “shutdown” in Washington. He attends every session, hoping for a resolution. . . but there is definitely a war going on in the nation’s capital.. But Cleaver is keeping the faith and still earning national respect, every day. Just like the Chiefs. No doubt about Sunday. Chiefs by 7.

Ike “Out of Control” Gardner: Ike is intelligent, committed but cantankerous as hell. He flatout disagrees with everybody, except his current and past St. James pastors, both Rev. and E-3. He did say a few nice things about Dontari Poe, the Chiefs defensive nose guard. But, he was Ol’ Ike when it came to the outcome, Tennessee by 3.

Lloyd Greenfield: Lloyd, unlike painful Ike, is a mild-mannered, thoughtful guy. He feels that educated people are more effective, when they share their talents with lesser educated people. This “team” approach will make the city, better for everyone. Further, he is pleased with the Chiefs’ team play and Jamaal Charles in particular. They will shine again in Tennessee Sunday. Chiefs by 7

Ollie Hubbard: Big Hub is one of Lincoln University’s greatest advocates. If Lincoln is having a fundraiser, Ollie will call you at 3 a.m., to remind you of your “forced” promise to buy a ticket to a Lincoln event. That dedication has earned him many friends. Just like his wisdom in supporting the Chiefs, this Sunday. Chiefs by 7

Jim “Granddad” Nunnelly: He is really gung-ho about this mental health and violence stuff. His family is running from him because he is urging them to get involved in this community issue. His grandson, Christian, says his grandfather is all about “liberating mental health”. In fact, he notes a mental uplift in the Chiefs. The special teams are leading the way., including this Sunday. Chiefs by 7

Ed West: He keeps wondering why local control of the police department keeps coming up. With people leading the board like Alvin Brooks, Ed asks “what’s the problem”? He is asking that about the Chiefs, as well. With special teams scoring points and getting good field position. . . what’s the problem?. . . thus, Chiefs by 10.

Many of our Monday Niters have written off the Chiefs for 2008. Larry Johnson hasn’t helped with his off-the-field shenanigans. And the lack of an NFL-status quarterback complicates an already dismal season. And to make matters worse, every one is asking who will the first to get the ax. . . Carl Peterson or Herm Edwards.

To Elize and Clyde in Oakland, I am ready to concede our bet and just not talk about the Chiefs anymore. So, we Monday Niters talked about the ensuing, history-making election.

DR. CHARLES ALLEN: Charles says he will vote early, on his way into work. . .Charles says his office staff can take as long as it takes to vote. . . is Chiefs are struggling right now, having only a 1-4 record. . .Charles just won’t give up. . .Chiefs by 3

PAUL BOOKER: Paul will be out of town on November 4. . . he is considering voting absentee on that day. . . Paul believes that the Chiefs are a dysfunctional family. . .Jets by 20.

CARL CLARK: Carl will vote in the mid-morning, avoiding the on-the-way-to-work rush. . .Carl will never vote again for those Chiefs. . . Jets by 20

BUBBA CLEAVER: Praised his fellow Monday Niters for being so knowledgeable about election stuff. . .he has helped Obama campaign in Minnesota and Georgia and is being asked to go to Ohio and Arizona soon. . .It IS written that the Chiefs will lose badly in New York. . . Jets by 12

IKE GARDNER: Ike first voted for Adlai Stevenson way back in 1956. . . he is guaranteeing that his adult children will vote for Obama. . . saying the Chiefs are gathering steam as the roll downhill...Jets by 35.

LLOYD GREENFIELD : Lloyd votes at Grace Point Church at 104th and Blue River Road. . . he will vote electronically. . .thinks we ought to plan a memorial services for the departed Chiefs. . .Jets by 20

OLLIE HUBBARD: Ollie is the only Monday Niter who has already voted. . . he voted absentee because of his leg disability. . .Ollie always votes for the team that the Chiefs are playing. . .this time, no exception. . . Jets by 40.

Interim Superintendent

And The Lowly Chiefs

The NFL season is finally here. After a threatened strike, a boatload of free agents, and a new set of feet, the Chiefs will open at Arrowhead on Sunday. And you will see folks going to early morning church services, in order to witness the kickoff game to the 2011 season. And like the tornado that hit Joplin and the hurricane Irene that racked the east coast, the Chiefs have a lot of damage to clean up. You know they released All Pro offensive lineman, Brian Waters. And Peanut Brittle Matt Cassel is ailing, as usual. Then, the Chiefs did not win a single pre-season game, this summer. That all adds up to another questionable season ahead, in spite of the fact the Chiefs made the playoffs last year.

In many ways, the Chiefs are like the Kansas City, Mo., School District. Every year, they come up with a different reason why any kind of success evades them. In fact, Big Bad John Covington balanced the budget but he did absolutely nothing to improve the test scores. He closed a lot of schools but did not open up any new academic trails. And once again, Booker says it is purely chaotic, down at 1211 McGee. No one knows what to do about it and that’s tragic. As expected each year, the enrollment, the energy and the ebullience decreases.

I suggest that at least the Board ought to hire an Associate Superintendent, whose only job is to monitor the academic progress of our children and keep the community updated on the accreditation status. This person should be a resident of Kansas City and have credibility in our community. This over-reliance on itinerant Superintendents is crazy. And we in this community can change that. We really can. All right, enough of that. Let’s return to talking about the Cheaps (I mean Chiefs).

Ike Gardner and Paul Booker are back at their old habits again. They can’t see the Chiefs beating anybody this year. After all, the Chiefs play New England, San Diego twice, Pittsburg, Minnesota, Indianapolis, and even Detroit. That’s what happens when a non-playoff-calibre teams make the playoffs, They suffer greatly the next year. But Carl Clark, Ollie Hubbard, and of course, Dr. Charles Allen believe the Chiefs will be “up” for the challenge and will win their first game, against Buffalo. So, like the School District, the Chiefs are steadily losing their support....both here and nationally.

What will it take to get it back for both of these also-rans?

It’s simple. Just do SOMETHING right. Anything. Is help on the way? As is our duty, we will monitor the progress and report to you each week. Just like we have been doing for forty two years now. Whew! Time passes, doesn’t it. Hint to the District and the Chiefs.

SPT - MND NITERS

Aaron Alexis, Mental Health Conference, And The Chiefs

Just like always, our country is faced with another mental health dilemma, Someone kills up a lot a people and we turn our attention to the crime, and not the events leading up to the horror. Thank God, we are going to have a conference here in Kansas City that will address the entire issue of mental health and mental illness.

Yes, this Saturday at the Downtown Marriott, everyday folks will have an opportunity to talk about this woefully overlooked and under-discussed field. In fact, about 50 people from the urban core of Kansas City are signed up to participate in the day-long conversation. It’s all about planning and for once, we will be at the table, before it happens.

Now, what does this all have to do with the now-winning Chiefs.

Well, mental health controls everything we do. . . everything we think. . . and everything we try to do. And the Chiefs have been in a deep depression, for a long, long time. And now it appears that they are pulling themsellves out of it. That’s just what we all have to do. Address squarely the problem we are facing. The Chiefs went out and got a quarterback, some offensive linemen and a new coach. . . to address the losing streak and loss of fans.

And they are gaining the fans back, with their early efforts in the season. Dr. Charles Allen, Paul Booker, Bubba Cleaver, Lloyd Greenfield, Jim Nunnelly, and Ed West predicted that the Chiefs would win against Philadelphia. Of course, that cantankerous Ike Gardner and that garrulous Carl Clark thought the Chiefs have not changed, from last season.

But face it, the depression is lifting. The Chiefs are talking and looking better these days. They are working hard at addressing the things that hurt them. Just like we as individuals should do.

As we must do from time to time, we must remove cewrtain people from our lives. . .so that we can do better. In fact, the Chiefs are a lesson in regaining their mental health.

So, Dr. Allen will be going to the conference on Saturday to talk about Alzheimer’s season. Jim Nunnelly will be addressing the stigma, associated with people who are struggling mentally. And Andrea Buford will be facilitating discussion tables, down at the Marriott. It will all be part of a historical time where we in the urban community, will be talking about the future and not arguing about the past.

Maybe the first topic of discussion will be. . . how did the Chiefs overcome their depression.

The answer is simple and complex. It took a lot of time, patience, and a lot of support from people who used to love them.

All of you are invited to the Marriott on Saturday. Come see how to deal with mental health issues. With you becoming the focal point. . . for your own mental health.

Spt - Monday Niters

Chiefs, Not At All Like Beyonce

Beyonce in her new album says “I woke up like this”. That means she was not taught, nor did she acquire any of her great talents. Inferentially, she is saying that she was “born” with such entirety. Let’s see how that applies to the Chiefs, after their rather surprising win over Miami. But before that, let’s record what happened over to Paul Booker’s house recently.

First, we talked about the upcoming community conversation about Health. Sponsored by the Healthcare Foundation, this big conversation will take place on October 11, down at the Sheraton Hotel in Crown Center. Everyone can come but you must register. More important, all of the Monday Niters will be there, expressing their views on behalf of the central city elderly. So, the guys just gave a sample of the things they will promote and say, during this high-tech conversation.

Dr. Charles Allen and Paul Booker want to see more health programs, addressing mental health and Alzheimer’s disease. And Ike Gardner, Lloyd Greenfield and Carl Clark expressed the critical need to address diabetes. They say that this diabetes thing is rampant and will speak collectively for those thousands of uncontrolled diabetics. And Ed West chimes in about the need for nursing home reform and the lack of adequate health insurance among our elderly brothers and sisters. In fact, the Monday Niters will be working with elderly organizations like the Palestine Senior Citizens Center to get their support. It is the goal to have fifty central city elderly persons at that conference. The business, at hand, is clear.

Speaking of business, the guys started reminiscing again. Last week, it was about health care providers. This year, they switched to entrepreneurs of the past.

Here are some of the names that emerged from the early Kansas City days:. G.L. Blankenship (beauty products); George and Ollie Gates (barbq); Madame C. J. Walker (cosmetics); Fannie Meeks (funeral services); Sarah Rector (real estate); Everett O’Neal (security); Earl Pitts (tire company); Leon Arnold (real estate); Chuck Wimes (mortgage lending); and Roy Walker (electrician). These brave men and women really set the tone for Kansas City’s rise to international fame and are the maverick heroes of our past.

Speaking of heroes, the Chiefs had several, last week in their win over Miami. Knile Davis ran wild, making us think that Jamaal Charles had better get well quick. And certainly, McKnight was a pleasant surprise, catching two touchdown passes. And the defense showed that there is some hope that they will jell, some day. But the real message is not at all like Beyonce’s. The Chiefs were not born, like they played Saturday. It is and has been a struggle, every step of the way. Eleven starters hurt. A bunch of undrafted no-names. A highly-paid but inconsistent quarterback. An ineffective offensive line. All of this adds up to a severe lack of “entirety”. Up till now, the Chiefs could and would not impress Beyonce, at all.

Now, Denver and Seattle have that Beyonce-like- entirety, displaying “seasoned excellence”, on every down and every Sunday. The Chiefs only have to dream about this kind of play. So let’s not get too happy over last Sunday’s win. That’s because the New England Patriots will be coming to town Monday, playing the Chiefs before a nationally televised audience. And they Patriots will be playing like Beyonce sings and dances, repeating to themselves often that “they woke up like this”.

Like Mila Banks, we all hope the Chiefs will continue upward. But who knows about those Chiefs? They are quite “sometimey” and they can change in a New York minute. But, here’s what the guys think will happen at Arrowhead on Monday night:

Spt- Monday Niters

George Jefferson, Fantasies And The 11-5 Chiefs

Do you remember “The Jeffersons”?

That was a tv show that appeared regularly on CBS, way back in the seventies. This was a Norman Lear production, that attempted to show that black people were “moving on up”. In fact, the show took place in the Jeffersons’ Fifth Avenue apartment, on the upper East Side of New York City. And since the prosperity was all new to the Jeffersons, the writers traced their humorous, everyday activities. Some of it was funny but the whole thing was filled with a lot of fantasy.

Speaking of fantasy, the Chiefs have been living on “Fantasy Island”. For the first nine games the Chiefs were the ‘talk of the sports world”. They were undefeated until they met the Payton-laced Denver Broncos. Then the Chargers. Then, the Broncos again. And just like that, the little “fantasy” bubble began to bust. In fact, these same Chiefs lost five of their next seven games, the latest being to the Chargers last Sunday. Talk about up and down. The Chiefs were living in a fantasy world and it all came tumbling down, causing many to mentally eliminate the Chiefs from further contention.

But is it over for the Chiefs? Now remember the Chiefs were 2-14 in 2013. And now they are in playoffs and have been there solidly for three weeks running. They didn’t need help from other teams, they clinched a spot and just waited. So, my point is that the “fantasy” continues, in spite of them losing the last third of the season. And last Sunday, they lost but they didn’t use twenty of the first twenty two starters. So, San Diego beat the second and third teamers of the Chiefs. Not Jamaal Charles or Tamba Hali or Derrick Johnson or Dwayne Bowe.

So what’s the point? The most salient point is that the Chiefs are still pursuing the NFL Super Bowl game.. Even Carl Clark says let’s support the Chiefs “for the heck of it”. As always, that Paul Booker and Ike Gardner are non-supportive, believing that the Chiefs will lose their first round playoff game to Indianapolis Colts, just like they did a couple of weeks ago.And for sure, that will be “it” for Kansas City and its fantasy season.

Hold up for a minute!. The Chiefs did put eight players on the All-Pro roster. And their coach, Andy Reid, will probably be Coach Of The Year. And some second teamers are going to playing a lot, come Sunday afternoon. Thereby, making the Chiefs even better prepared for the upcoming game.

Bottom line, like the Jeffersons. . . the Chiefs are “moving on up”. They finally got a piece of the NFL pie. Now Ike will probably predict against the Chiefs. But you all know Ike. He was wrong eleven of sixteen times this year. That means he wasn’t right, most of the time, about the Chiefs. He is not the “Holy Grail” of predicting football games.

So, let’s get behind our hometown team and maybe, just maybe….we can “move further on up” to the East side and take on New England and then onto the West side to take on Denver. Wait. Wait. Wait. I’ve drifted back into Fantasy Land.

For right now, Chiefs over the Colts. (We hope).

Spt- Monday Niters (Shorter Version)

First Samuel, Chapter 17

The Bible, literature, television and everyday life are full of examples of how the unexpected victor rises, in spite of the odds. Well, that theme is appropriate for the youthful Chiefs as they take on the long-respected Indianapolis Colts. Yea, we know about the older, more war-like “Philistines”. There’s Manning and his passing partner, Reggie Wayne. And then on Kansas City’s side, there are the “Kids R Us”. And in “David and Goliath” fashion, these two teams will meet on Sunday, at noon, in Indianapolis. And like David, the Chiefs only have a sling shot, and a bag of five stones to throw at last year’s AFC champion. Those five stones are Hali, McCluster, Johnson, Charles, and Jones.

It begs the question....can the Chiefs (fresh off a bye week) beat the Colts (fresh off a butt-kicking from Jacksonville)? The answer is “yes they can”. But, is it realistic? Yes! But, is likely? NO! And the Monday Niters line up just like those who predicted that Goliath would destroy the little kid, named David. Let’s look in and see what these “haters” think about Sunday’s game.

THE VENERABLE DR. CHARLES ALLEN: Doc is truly a bible reader. He knows that the odds are against the Chiefs’ “kids” But, he also knows that everyone thought that Goliath would destroy David. Doc says repeatedly that the Chiefs are better than most people think. Not a hater at all. CHIEFS by 10.

THE APOSTLE PAUL BOOKER: Booker has consistently “hated on” the Chiefs. And you can’t blame him for the Chiefs have been “shaky”, as all of us were when we were young. But, Booker says the “impossible” will never happen in Peyton’s Place. Thus, he sees Goliath as the winner, on Sunday. COLTS by 14

THE COOL CARL CLARK. . . Carl is not a hater and he is not a loyalist, either. He’s on the fence a lot, often going with and against the Chiefs. Yet, he seems open to the possibility that the Chiefs may be a developing team that has not played a tough team yet. The Colts are that “tough” team. COLTS by 14

CONGRESSMAN “REV” CLEAVER: Definitely on the campaign trails these days, hoping to be re-elected to the United States Congress. In fact, he created a “preaching songbirds” program that will feature local ministers who sing to their congregations, as well as preach. So forgive him if he fails to think clearly about the Chiefs and their new rise to prominence. He’s been too busy campaigning and has allowed Ike and Booker to over-influence his thinking. He says the Chiefs are better, but not going to have a chance in Peyton Place. COLTS by 10.

IKE “PAW-PAW” GARDNER: A Chiefs “hater” from way back. While intelligent, Ike just doesn’t see the light. Without even thinking it through, he says the Chiefs are leading the NFL’s weakest division. He says the only way the Chiefs can win on Sunday is for the Colts to forfeit the game. Whew! He adds the Chiefs have too many “kids” on their 3-0 team. Regretfully he is taking GOLIATH AND THE COLTS by 17.

USUALLY LOYAL LLOYD GREENFIELD: Lloyd has been talk to Ikd and that Booker a lot lately, indicating that he is wavering in his belief about the Chiefs. Usually, he is fearlessly backing the Chiefs. But, like the Goliath crowd, he is changing his vote this time and going with the Colts. He believes the Chiefs “are still in their infancy” and cannot beat the Chiefs, at this time. COLTS by 6

OLLIE “CNN” HUBBARD: Any kind of news is Ollie’s business, whether true or untrue. And he thinks there will be “good news”, coming out of Indianapolis on Sunday. As a member of Ebenezer AME Church, Ollie believes the Chiefs will sink a stone into the “forehead” of the Colts. A former hater, Ollie is now a believer/loyalist. CHIEFS by 3.

JIM “GRAND DAD” NUNNELLY: Always supporting the youngsters. He feels the Chiefs are re-tooled and have new skills, at every position i.e. running back, defensive line, linebackers, secondary, and even the offensive line and special teams. If there is a weakness, it is the “forced bye week” that came when the Chiefs were “rising fast”. Currently, the only undefeated team, the Chiefs will use their “rocks” wisely and overcome the “sluggish” Colts. Face it yall, Goliath and the Colts are getting older and slower and showing signs of demise. CHIEFS by 3.

ED “THE GEE” WEST: Ed is one of the few “senior citizens” who always packs his i-phone and laptop computer wherever he goes. And he settles many an argument by just speaking into his phone. His quiet demeanor serves him well for he usually goes with the facts and never allows the “haters” to get to him. Though he knows the Chiefs can throw running backs at the Colts all day Sunday. And he knows that the Chiefs are vastly improved over years past. But, he thinks the Chiefs don’t have enough to beat the Colts. COLTS all the way.

ERIC “ERICA’S DAD” WESSON: Eric has not drank the Chiefs’ kool aid yet. Yes they look good defensively in a lot of areas. He feels they have not been tested yet. San Diego is one dimensional without their work horse running back LaDainian Tomlinson who they let go for some unknown reason. If I were the owner I would fire everyone who had something to do with that deal. Then they beat “no diminsional” Cleveland Browns and San Francisco. payton Manning in the man inspite of several losses early in the season. Once they start rolling they may win 12 games in a row including Sunday’s slacking of the Chiefs by 9.

Final Note: Everyone knows there is always a chance for the underdog. Many of us have prevailed, when all the odds were totally not in our favor. And that will be the motivation that the Chiefs will take into Indianapolis, Sunday. Clearly, we are living in “changing times”. Who would have thought we would ever have a black man in the White House? And likewise, who would have thought the Chiefs would be undefeated and positioning themselves to become a contender? Those of you who line up with the “haters”, read First Samuel, Chapter 17, verse 49. Enough said.

I was a firefighter in this community for over 30 years. I have seen men and women perform under the worst of disasters. Thus, knowing about courage and skill is something I have come to expect from every professional, especially those in such high-paid, visible sports professions. Something is definitely wrong with our team, y’all! Maybe it’s the management who seems to say it is “my way or the highway”. Or maybe it is the offensive line, who continually allow the quarterback and running backs no opportunity at all. I know for sure that the top management is the “weakest link”.

But that is not happening with our Chiefs and the result is a winless season for the Chiefs, joining St. Louis , Tennessee , and Tampa Bay . But after seeing the game against Dallas , there is some hope, especially among the defensive players. While I haven’t given up, I do believe they will lose again on Sunday against Washington.

Where is the development plan?

As one can discern, the Monday Niters are divided about what team will win on Sunday. Enough said about that. Ike and Booker are the season’s best predictors. Enough said about that, too. But rather than write an organized, thoughtful column, let’s just look at some Super Bowl trivia and potpourri (just miscellaneous stuff). This will be particularly helpful to those of you who are casual football fans and look to our column for helpful hints.

Here goes:

•Janet Jackson has been replaced by Paul McCartney as the halftime keynote.

•It's been 24 years, since the Eagles landed at the Super Bowl. Dick Vermeil was the coach, then.

•Jacksonville is the smallest and newest city to host a Super Bowl. The site is Alltell Stadium.

•New England will be in the Super Bowl for the third time in four years. Tom Brady, Patriot quarterback, was named MVP in the two games won by the Patriots.

•Most of the chatter has been about whether Terrell Owens will play. Chances are he will suit up and star. The Pro Bowler caught 14 touchdown passes in 14 games.

•Tony Gonzalez, Chiefs' All-Pro tight end, says New England will win. Also, Renee Gardner, Ike's daughter, believes in the Patriots, as well.

•It was Super Bowl 22, in 1988 when Doug Williams, first black quarterback to start a Super Bowl, just showed out in Washington’s victory over John Elway and the Denver Broncos. The score was 42-10, with Doug throwing for 4 touchdowns.

•New England safety, Rodney Harrison is considered the “dirtiest” player in the NFL. He has been fined over $350,000 for his vicious hits on receivers.

•Lloyd and Geneva Greenfield will host the Monday Niters and their guests at their Birchwood subdivision home for the annual Super Bowl party. Lloyd is a real estate advisor and Geneva is a renown storyteller.

Chiefs Are Not Pearls. . . More Like Costume Jewelry…

Someone once said that “a pearl is not a pearl, until it comes out of its shell”. That should have become the theme for our Monday night session, down at the Juke House. Congressman Cleaver hosted the event Monday and the evening was full of history lessons. That meant that everyone was cordial to each other (being the first meeting of the season). Then, things turned sour and we began to verbally assault the Chiefs. However before that we spent considerable time, talking about the domestic violence in the NFL, a revered teacher in the Kansas City Public Schools history, and then, we lit in on the Chiefs.

First, about the domestic violence. This subject arose because the news media is going crazy, talking about the elevator video showing Ray Rice beating his girlfriend. Under the new NFL policy, he will be suspended for six games and if a second event occurs, he could be banned “for life”. This week the Baltimore Ravens cut Rice’s contract and the NFL suspended him indefinitely. Let’s go on.

Secondly, we reminisced. Mainly about the “good old days”, when education was truly our number one priority in Kansas City. Jimmy Ray Hubbard said during a recent visit that at one time “Lincoln High School had more master’s level and PhD teachers than any other school. That’s why the guys, especially Ike, Carl, Ed and Lloyd saluted Dr. Jeremiah Cameron, English teacher at Lincoln in the forties and fifties and sixties. Sadly but understandably, some short-sighted people labeled the late Dr. Cameron as “arrogant”. But always lauded for his teaching skills and his relentless efforts to fully educate our children. And to Jimmy Ray Hubbard’s point, he had a master’s in English from the University of Chicago and an earned PhD from Michigan State University. Now, this “pearl” is/was definitely out of his shell. In fact, it makes us all nostalgic….wishing for the “good old days”, when the quality of our educational system was never in question.

Lastly, we turned to another “pearl” that is nowhere near “out of the shell”. Yes, we all chimed in our luck-less, lifeless Kansas City Chiefs, After the shellacking they received Sunday by Tennessee, Ed West quickly said the Chiefs may only win two games this year. Booker, who is really happy these days, said the Chiefs are not worth a (four letter word). And we were all uniform in believing that Alex Smith is more like “costume jewelry” than a pearl. And everyone is still wondering why Jamaal Charles remained in his shell, all afternoon.

At some point, we all became redundant and as usual, we began to repeat ourselves. So real quickly, the question was asked. . . “how will the Chiefs do against Denver on Sunday”. Well, I’ve never witnessed such laughter in my life. It was like Kat Williams or Kevin Hart had told a funny joke.

Now folks, Denver is saturated with “all kinds of pearls”….Manning, a great offensive line, Vonn Miller, an effective secondaryand on and on. So except for two loyalists, all of us believe the Chiefs will be murdered by the Broncos.

In fact, the mood switched from speaking highly about Dr. Jeremiah Cameron to a relentless cascade of tears for the sad Chiefs. And we left there, with the Chiefs’ pearls, still in their shell……

Dr. Charles Allen. A hope and a prayer. . . . Chiefs by 3

Paul Booker: K.C. cannot tame Denver . . . Denver by 14.

Carl Clark: K.C. has no offensive line . . . Denver by 21.

Spt - Monday Niters

Thank You, Lisa Page

Surely, every one of our readers have heard the smashing, gospel hit song, “I Just Want To Thank You”.

With that in mind, we, Monday Niters, are in a “Thanksgiving spirit” and want to lace our column with both the song and the season in mind, Thus, we are asking the question.....what player should the Chiefs be most thankful for.

And then, we are asking each Monday Niter, what he is personally thankful for. And in between the lines, we will gently place a few facts, along side our prediction of the Seattle-Kansas City game on Sunday. Actually, after beating Arizona at home last Sunday, the Chiefs, at 6-4, have a better record than the Seahawks, at 5-5.

And like old times, the Chiefs are undefeated at home. Conversely, except for Cleveland, the Chiefs have lost four games on the road. Unjustly, the “haters” were not courteous enough to call us and congratulate us “loyalists” on our predictions, last week. But, the “haters” are like the “tea party folks”, they just want to hate.

Dr. Charles Allen: Doc says the Chiefs ought to be thankful for the maturation and development of Jamaal Charles, their third year running back. And being the gentleman he is, he says he personally is thankful for his fine family, his health, and his many patients, who have placed great faith in his professional capabilities. Chiefs by 10

Paul Booker: Fresh from celebrating his two birthdays, Booker is thankful that he hasn’t been run out of town for his many disloyal predictions, against the Chiefs. However, he is feeling better about the Chiefs, especially since the arrival of Dwayne Bowe, the developing wide receiver from Louisiana State university. While a “hater-at-heart”, Paul truly loves his wife, kids and grandkids with unspeakable joy and admiration. And sometimes he even goes to church at Mount Pleasant. But, not enough to believe in the Chiefs this week. Seattle by 7

Carl Clark: Is really thankful for he will celebrate his 51st wedding anniversary on Friday. Thus, he has ceaseless admiration for his wife and family. And as for the Chiefs, they ought to be thankful that they have Jamaal Charles, who has had five 100-yard-plus rushing games. Carl also believes those extra- long practice sessions helped the Chiefs regain their AFC West lead. No longer a hater. Chiefs by 3

Congressman Bubba Cleaver: man of the cloth, he is so thankful to have a “fabulous” family and an “unusally high” number of friends and supporters, And he says the Chiefs should thank God for the best, complementary running-back duo in the NFL...Charles and Jones. After Sunday’s victory, Rev. is moving away from Ike and Booker’s influence and standing on his own. Chiefs by 6

Issac Gardner, Jr.: Yes, Ike was named after his “Chef-on-the-train” dad. Now deceased, Mr. Gardner would often cook wild-game dinners for us guys and our families. Thus, Ike is thankful for God and his family. And while still mad at the Chiefs for now over 40 years, he says they should be thankful for two-time, All-Pro, Brian Waters. Now in his 11th season, Waters has lent stability to a “sometimey” offensive line. But, that’s not enough for Ike to reduce his “hating”. Seattle by 10

Lloyd Greenfield: Lloyd is thankful for “life”, in general. Certainly he includes his family and his many friends in that statement. And a month ago, he veered from his usual mild-manners and chided Dwayne Bowe for his mediocre play, Now he apologizes after seeing Bowe operate these last few games, especially against Arizona and Denver. Because of his prolific fair-mindedness, most folks refer to Lloyd as a “gentleman’s gentleman”. Chiefs by 7

Ollie Hubbard: You remember last week, Ollie went on and on about his “earth angel” wife, Sandra. And it wasn’t even Thanksgiving. But, this week he is adding to his list his two “classy” daughters and his brilliant granddaughter, Karrington. Plus, the many who depend on him for local and national news. And like Lloyd, he, too, feels Bowe has arrived and just might make All-Pro, Especially if the Chiefs luck up and win the West. . . Keep being thankful, Ollie. Chiefs by 3

Jim Nunnelly: Feels that the Chiefs defense, led by Tamba Hali, is grossly over-looked. Hali is only second to Derrick Thomas and Neil Smith, when it comes to forcing fumbles. And yes, look for the fifth year linebacker to be All-Pro this year. Personally, Grand Dad is thankful for his wife, two adult children and the five grandchildren (who have made major career decisions this year). And he also is thankful for the Call, the Carter Broadcast Group and Swope Health Services for standing behind the teenagers in our city. And he looks for Hali and the Rockets to stop Seattle cold. Chiefs by 14

Ed West: Wouldn’t you know that this computer genius would make his wife and family, along with his health, as his most-thankful-for part of his life? That’s well-rounded Ed. And while each of us picked a Chiefs’ player, Ed selected Romeo Crennel, the defensive coordinator. Ed says the Chiefs were just lucky to have landed a defensive leader who has been to the Super Bowl five times, in various capacities. So, Ed is full of thanks and geeky information. . . .and surely believes Kansas City will win its seventh game against Seattle. Chiefs by 10

Final note: This week, being Thanksgiving and all, we are certainly thankful for the Call and their staff, and to each of you for allowing us to be a part of your life, especially during the football season. For over 40 years now, we have gathered each Monday night and discussed the ways of the world, our families, and occasionally football. Never has it been entirely about football, though we each love the game. Really, it has always been about friendship, camaraderie and good, clean fun. And as mad as we might make each other sometimes, we have never gone home angry or unforgiven.

Again, thanks to each of you and especially Lisa Page for reminding all of us to be thankful everyday, not just on Thanksgiving.

Spt - Monday Niters

Brandon Lloyd, Alicia Keys, And The Chiefs

Did you hear about the butt-kicking New England handed the league-leading Houston Texans, last Monday night. We gathered over to Ed West’s, to watch what we thought would be a good game. It didn’t happen. The Brady Bunch slaughtered the Texans, 42-14, with Brady tossing four touchdown passes.

On the bright spot is that game was Kansas City’s own, Brandon Lloyd. We remember Brandon’s high school days out at Blue Springs. From there, he went on to star at wide receiver, at the University of Illinois. And then, he has caught passes for the Denver Broncos, the St. Louis Rams and now the New England Patriots. Known for his acrobatic catches, this guy is “on fire”. It is not every receiver who has the best passer in NFL history as his passing mate. Look for more.

Speaking of being “on fire”. Have you heard Alicia Keys’ latest CD, “This Girl Is On Fire”. It is selling like wildfire. If you have seen the video, she paints the story of a young woman, taking on the struggles and joys of life. Not to be outdone, she has to be “on fire”, just to make it. This song has rapidly become the spokes-song for millions of young women, adrift in this world. But, to me, her CD begs the question. . . who is NOT “on fire”. You guessed it. It’s the Kansas City Chiefs. Two buckets of gasoline and a box of matches won’t light up the Chiefs, these days.

They have been down so long that “getting up” don’t even cross their minds. With only two wins again eleven losses, the network commentators don’t even comment on the score, demonstrating their neglect of and disdain for these Chiefs. In fact, talking about the Chiefs was so dull. . . that we found ourselves talking about Robert Griffin III. . . the old time Georgy Burger. . . how integration killed the economic spirit of 18th and 12th streets. . . Tom Brady’s record of a passing touchdown for forty two consecutive games. . . Charles Kynard, premier organist who played at the Blue Room in the sixties...how the post office, the school district and the automotive industry were the plum jobs of the ‘50s, ‘60s, and ‘70s. . . how Carl Clark ignored the state-line boundaries and found Mary Lynn in Kansas City, Kas.

Actually, our discussion had more “fire” than the Chiefs have. In fact, our discussion soon landed into a healthy respect of years-gone-by. When we were all in one neighborhood and acted and thought unity. When our business owners were our neighbors and our churches were our uniform resource. Yes, when the Chiefs could walk on water, while being “on fire” at the same time. When being from Kansas City was something to behold. . . not to silently mention, in passing. Where did it all go? We don’t know but we wish we had those same pride-filled moments back. And it won’t get any better out in Oakland, this coming Sunday.

The Monday Niters have given up on the Chiefs and all that they stand/stood for. Bottom line, the “fire next time” is a long time away.

Lloyd Greenfield: Chiefs are a broken vessel. Raiders by 14

Ollie Hubbard: I’m praying at Ebenezer. Toss-up

Jim Nunnelly: Fire is out for Romeo. Raiders by 7

Ed West: No Bowe, no go! Raiders by 10

Monday

Okay, the Chiefs won one game.

It just so happened on national television and it was raining and the team we beat just happened to have won the AFC West Division last year. And for the first time in years, the Chiefs were part of the highlight reels on ESPN, as a positive. But just as sure as Todd Haley is not going to smile, the jury is still out on the Chiefs, at least the offense. Speaking of offense, did you see Matt Cassel grab the game ball, as if he had something to do with the victory over San Diego? Make no mistake. . . that game ball should have gone to the defense, or the punt returner or the kickoff returner or Jamal Charles. Definitely not the quarterback.

As we look ahead to Sunday, let’s see what the Monday Niters think about the upcoming game against Cleveland, in Cleveland.

DR. CHARLES ALLEN: Monday night’s win over the Chargers was NOT a fluke. Though improvement is still needed, the Chiefs will overtake the Browns by 10 points.

THE APOSTLE PAUL BOOKER: I’m not that confident about the Chiefs, at least not yet. The Chiefs have gotten us all excited before, only to let us down. It is my firm belief that the Chiefs cannot and won’t beat Cleveland. End of story.

COOL CARL CLARK: Unlike Booker, Carl believes the Chiefs CAN beat the Browns, if for no other reason that any team can beat another team on any given Sunday. The Chiefs played good defense but the Chiefs’ quarterback is lacking an awful lot.

CONGRESSMAN EMANUEL CLEAVER: From this point forward, the Chiefs will be measured by speed. They got lots of speed, on the line, in the backfield, on special teams. With that abundance of speed, they can and will beat Cleveland.

CANTANKEROUS IKE GARDNER: The only place Ike is nice to people is out at a restaurant on 63rd. There he is treated like royalty and he eats it up. But, his distrusting dislike for the Chiefs While everyone else is raving about the Chiefs’ defense, Ike says the “the defense let us down”. Thus, Cleveland will beat our Chiefs on Sunday, according to Ike.

LOYAL LLOYD GREENFIELD: He agrees that the win over San Diego was a fluke. But, he was glad to see the Chiefs win something. And for the record, he is backing the Chiefs this Sunday against the Browns. Can we trust your judgement, Lloyd?

OLLIE “KNOWS EVERYTHING” HUBBARD: Ollie is happy with the Chiefs. You know he is part of the Ike and Booker thing that goes against the Chiefs. But this time, Ollie joins up with the believers and picks Kansas City over Cleveland.

JIM “GRAND DAD” NUNNELLY: Matt Cassel ain’t worth $60 million, y’all. In fact, we would have killed San Diego with Brodie Croyle at quarterback. He is drinking from wells that he didn’t dig. But look for local writers and broadcasters to continue to make hi m an “unhung hero”. The only thing that didn’t work for the Chiefs was the offense. And guess who was the quarterback. Get rid of him and the Chiefs will soar. But, special teams are enough to beat Cleveland on Sunday.

EASY ED WEST: Joining the believers is Ed West. He flip-flops back and forth sometimes. But, this time, Ed is onto something. He thinks the Chiefs are young, hungry and motivated on both offense and defense. Thus, his vote goes to the Chiefs to outclass Crennel’s old team, the Browns.

ERIC “ERICA’S DAD” WESSON: There is an ole’ sayin’ “Even a hobo gets lucky and gets a steak every now and then if he hangs out in the right places.” That was what I was thinking in the Chiefs Monday victory, but it will be short lived. The bottom line is the Chiefs lack the offensive players to be a household name. They won’t be able to score enough to beat Cleveland. Season record 4-12.

FINAL NOTE: The haters (Booker and that Ike) are the only ones, not believing in the Chiefs. The believers are strong in their belief and feel that prayer has worked well for the Chiefs. It certainly was not the coaching or the quarterback. So after leaving early church on Sunday, look for the Chiefs to be 2-0, by three o’clock. Believe, y’all.

“Vicissitudes”

This word means the expected changes and variations. The weather and the stock market are examples of vicissitudes. Now, our beloved Chiefs have adopted that same philosophy. They start out strong, scoring many times on the first possession; then, they fade a little in the middle; and just plain peter out in the end. So many times lately, we Monday Niters have witnessed that. And because of the Chiefs’ likely outcomes, we spent last Monday night, talking about movies, rather than the Chiefs. While some of us are in denial, we are all disillusioned optimists. Consequently, our left-over verve from last year is gone and all we can talk about now is classic, big-screen favorites like

“Imitation of Life” and “One Eyed Jacks” and “The Godfather” and on and on. For 35 non-stop years, we have ceaselessly talked about the Chiefs and Monday Night Football. And after the New Orleans debacle, we might as well change our interests to movies. For there is not much to talk about these days, when it comes to our Chiefs.

But, wait! Didn’t the Chiefs beat Baltimore, earlier this year, on a Monday night? Yes. And didn’t we once beat Buffalo, a Super Bowl contender, here at Arrowhead a decade ago? Well, doggone it. The Chiefs, after many vicissitudes, might just topple the high-flying New England Patriots. Maybe. Or else we’ll change our name.

African Cabbage, Mrs. Myla Banks, And The Chiefs

We, Monday Niters, know that when we go to Dr. Allen’s house, there will be “something new”. Of course, Dr. Allen, a long-respected dentist, fashions himself as a culinary artist. He thrives on finding different foods to serve us.

This week it was African cabbage, merlot-induced gravy, a medley of beans, macaroni with ham and peas, fried chicken strips and Yorkshire pig feet. Yes, we had pig feet and it was presented as a delightful delicacy.

Of course, Dr. Allen recognized the essential contributions of his daughter, Tee and his wife, Beverly. Commonly called the “A Team”, they helped us celebrate the Chiefs’ victory over Green Bay.

It certainly helped that Dr. Allen was the only Monday Niter to pick the Chiefs over Green Bay. So he was all smiles. Even overly-mean Ike Gardner was grinning.

That’s because Mrs. Myla Banks, a long-time educator in the Kansas City Missouri School District, actually phoned Ike. That shows you how nice Mrs. Banks is because no one calls Ike just to chat. Right there in the middle of the game, he and Mrs. Banks talked about old times and she chided Ike for being so hard on the Chiefs. In fact, she flat-out told him she was picking the Chiefs over Oakland, Saturday at Arrowhead. Of course, everyone saluted Mrs. Banks because she has been one of our most faithful readers of our column. That’s a blessing because Mrs. Banks still can be seen around town, talking to the owner of the Chiefs or conversing at the annual NAACP luncheon.

Thanks, Mrs. Banks, but that wasn’t all that happened.

Paul “Hater of All Haters” Booker actually made a confession, stopping all the usual noise. And you will have to read his comments below to find out exactly what he said to “blow us all away”. We were floored and most of us went home, knowing that Booker had been “touched” by something. The 19-14 victory over Green Bay must have surprised him big-time. And so it was with the Chiefs.

The Chiefs could not believe it, themselves. Green Bay came to town, undefeated and expected to just beat up the lowly-Chiefs and move on toward the Super Bowl. But, Tambi Hali and the defense gave the offense some room. And the new quarterback, Orton, looked like he “done found him a job”. When was the last time you saw a Chiefs’ quarterback zip a pass between two defenders? Face it, Orton is the “O” factor and should be expected to help the Chiefs this week. And if things work out against Oakland, then the new Chiefs with the “O’ factor will return to Denver, against the team that cut him four weeks ago. Speaking of Oakland, will the Chiefs make a serious run for the playoffs? Let’s see what the Monday Niters think. Now, be sure to look for what Booker said.

Dr. Charles Allen: The new coach, Romeo Crennel, has created a new culture in the Chiefs’ organization. That culture led to a big, unexpected showing against Green Bay. The formidable Packers were not looking for a tough game against the Chiefs. But the Chiefs came through, just like Charles said they would. Chiefs by 10

Paul “the Converted Apostle” Booker: Booker says the change in attitude among the players is remarkable. They just wouldn’t play for Todd the Tyrant.

Like Patti LaBelle, the Chiefs now have a “new attitude”. And because of that, Booker is picking the Chiefs over Oakland.

Do you believe it?

He is parting waves with “that” Ike and has accepted the Chiefs as winners. Praise the Lord. Chiefs by 7

Carl Clark: Carl still thinks the Chiefs were lucky last Sunday, against Green Bay. Even though the Chiefs controlled the ball longer, sacked Aaron Rodgers several times, and even scored on two long drives.

Still, Carl is not convinced and he has now taken on Booker’s old role, as a “hater”. Oakland by 7

Congressman Cleaver: Cleaver is still in Washington, trying to get the Republicans to pass a tax-cut bill for the middle class. In fact, they are about to make “Rev.” mad. That’s because Rev. wants all of us to pay less payroll taxes and the Republicans are holding it up. And it is driving Rev insane with this kind of duplicity. Anyway, Rev is convinced the Chiefs played well and will probably do the same against the Raiders. . . so. . . Chiefs by 7

Ike “Oldest Grench Alive” Gardner: Ike enjoyed talking to Mrs. Myla Banks. He even smiled a time or two, during the ten-minute conversation. That made him say later that he was glad that dumb-ass Haley was no longer coaching the Chiefs. And he kind of sighed when he learned that his long-time-hater-ally, Booker had been converted to the Chiefs. That made him mad and he again denounced the Chiefs. . .what’s new?. . .Oakland by 10

Lloyd Greenfield: Lloyd says that Green Bay just under-estimated the Chiefs. It is refreshing to see the Chiefs playing as a team. They have recovered quickly from “Haley-itis”, which led them to mess up and not score. Lloyd is cautiously optimistic about the Chiefs winning the next two games. . .but starting Saturday. . . it is the. . . Chiefs by 3

Ollie “Dr. Death” Hubbard: Just the other day, Applejack Matthews commented that Ollie is among the world’s most informed obituary experts. And Ollie is not yet sounding the death alarm for the Chiefs. He thinks that this is just the beginning of a “new day” for the Chiefs. He is sending a loud message to his cousin, Faye, in Oakland. Chiefs by 5

Jim “Grand Dad” Nunnelly: There was a song by the Persuaders, called “ A Thin Line Between Love And Hate”. That’s how I feel about the Chiefs...you like ‘em one minute and hate ‘em the next. But last Sunday, no one hated the Chiefs. People everywhere were praising them, just like the were the new “David”, slaying Goliath Green Bay. More important, Romeo Crennel looks like he “done found a job too”. But he gotta win Christmas Eve against Oakland and New Year’s Day out at Denver. . . he will, at least this Saturday at Arrowhead. Chiefs by 6

Ed “Always Right” West: Ed never got over hiring a golf coach to coach pro football. You will remember Haley was a golf coach at one time and Ed has never supported him. But Ed is quick to praise Crennell for his philosophy includes both the offense and defense. Coming from a real football player, Ed is again right, especially about Romeo’s Christmas gift to the fans. Chiefs by 7

Eric “Last Second Christmas Shopper” Wesson: It is just hard to gauge what the Chiefs are going to do. They play so inconsistent. They allow Miami, which had not one any games, come in and beat them soundly. Then they send Green Bay packing in a game they really had no chance, on paper, of winning. But, I suppose a quarterback and coach will make a difference.

The Chiefs, will beat the Raiders by 10. Get Romeo’s three-year contract ready.

Note: Ed West just might have a 100 percent rating. 100 percent wrong. If the Chiefs win against San Diego, Ed West would have predicted all the games, inaccurately. The Chiefs started off 0-3 and now, have won four in a row. Wow, how unpredictable can a team get?

It reminds me of a story of a young minister, who was scheduled to preach his very first sermon. When it was his turn to preach, he strutted up to the podium, absolutely certain that he could move this church to previously-unreached heights. Arrogantly, he read the scriptures. And he shook his head, as if he knew the entire Bible and its meaning, by heart. And rather than slowly engage the congregation, he chose to talk down to them, as if they were second graders. He went on like this for what seemed like an eternity. However, the more he spoke, the more he realized that he was not getting or going anywhere, with this high-and-mighty approach. And slowly but reluctantly, he became more humble, as he read all the unresponding faces in the congregation. Feeling the rejection, he was almost in tears, as he prematurely began to close his botched sermon. And finally, he sat down, almost in shame. For he knew he had failed, miserably.

Embarrassed beyond belief after the services, he stood near the pulpit, head hung low. Then, an old man walked up to him and said, “Son, if you had got up, like you sat down; then, you could have sat down, like you got up.”

The moral of the story is that the Chiefs did not do that. They started off humbly and grew to become the winner they were supposed to become. Let’s give a big cheer to our Chiefs, for having come through, in the end.

Chatter, Conversation And Commentary

“Vicissitudes”

This word means the expected changes and variations. The weather and the stock market are examples of vicissitudes. Now, our beloved Chiefs have adopted that same philosophy. They start out strong, scoring many times on the first possession; then, they fade a little in the middle; and just plain peter out in the end. So many times lately, we Monday Niters have witnessed that. And because of the Chiefs’ likely outcomes, we spent last Monday night, talking about movies, rather than the Chiefs. While some of us are in denial, we are all disillusioned optimists. Consequently, our left-over verve from last year is gone and all we can talk about now is classic, big-screen favorites like

“Imitation of Life” and “One Eyed Jacks” and “The Godfather” and on and on. For 35 non-stop years, we have ceaselessly talked about the Chiefs and Monday Night Football. And after the New Orleans debacle, we might as well change our interests to movies. For there is not much to talk about these days, when it comes to our Chiefs.

But, wait! Didn’t the Chiefs beat Baltimore, earlier this year, on a Monday night? Yes. And didn’t we once beat Buffalo, a Super Bowl contender, here at Arrowhead a decade ago? Well, doggone it. The Chiefs, after many vicissitudes, might just topple the high-flying New England Patriots. Maybe. Or else we’ll change our name.

Editor, THE CALL:

Each week during football season, I look forward to reading the “Monday Niters” predictions and the commentary by Jim “Granddad” Nunnelly.

Mr. Nunnelly’s commentary in a recent “CALL” issue was particularly interesting as he told a little about each man in the “Monday Nite Footballers group.”

Nunnelly is an excellent writer. He knows how to make words on a page come alive.

Even though the “Monday Niters” lead varied lives with different professions, they enjoy commonalities that make them a cohesive group of remarkable black men.

In a recent write-up, Nunnelly described these grandfathers of the group from Allen to West as: extraordinary, fantastic, effective, proud, steadfast, remarkable, complete, and encouraging. For what more could one ask?

Monday Niters, “Keep on keeping on.” BRAVO!!.

Terry Strother

Burlington, Iowa

COMMENTARY, CONVERSATION AND CHATTER

Well, now. There are no “No. 1s” in the Final Four this year. Not since the eighties has such a No. 1-seed been playing for College Basketball’s championship in Indianapolis. Everybody is just gone wild over George Mason, the tiny little school from nearby Washington, D.C. These Patriots have beaten North Carolina, Michigan State, and Connecticut to get to play at the “big dance”. And it is true that this team has come back from deficits and played the kind of defense that only championship teams play. And everybody is now aware of just who and what George Mason university is. Appropriately called “the Cinderella team”, George Mason will play for but not become the Champion. That title will go to Shaq’s old school, Louisiana State university. Do look for a good matchup between Thomas and Davis.

SPT- Monday Niters

Flip Wilson, Old Sayings And The 11-4 Chiefs

You all remember Flip Wilson. He had his own television show, way back in the sixties. In fact, he was one of the first African American comedians to have a major white following. Most people will remember him as “Geraldine”, a cross-dressed character he created for the show. Everyone like ol’ Flip and he sure did a lot to reduce racial tensions, nationwide. Everyone waited for and watched Flip Wilson do his thing on Sunday evenings.

Speaking of doing things, the Chiefs failed to win, out at Arrowhead this last Sunday. But don’t fret too much because they will be still be playing after they play Sunday against the Chargers.. That simply means they have played well enough and won enough games to “qualify” for the Super Bowl chase.

However, two things. They have to win to stay in the mix. And secondly, they have to play on the road. Now, remember when the Chiefs won the Super Bowl after the 1969 season, they were in the same position. They were a wild card entry, which means they can pursue the Super Bowl….but they have to play on the road. If you want to know more about the only Super Bowl the Chiefs have played in, just ask Ike Gardner. He is still mad because the Chiefs have never regained the respect they once had, when they won the “big game” against the Joe Kapp and the Minnesota Vikings.

Speaking of Ike and his cantankerous ways, I owe him somewhat of an apology. You will remember last week, I talked about how Ike had consistently beat up on the Chiefs. In spite of them winning eleven games this year, he still insists they are a mediocre team at best. Truthfully, they are much more than they were last year. But last Sunday against Indianapolis , they stunk. I mean less than mediocre. After the first series touchdown, the Chiefs went downhill, the rest of the game. Thatcaused national commentators to talk badly about the Chiefs, as well. They say that the Chiefs can’t win against teams with winning tradition and records.

That sounds like what Ike has been saying. Really, I don’t mind what Ike says. I just don’t like the way he says it. There’s no love or loyalty in his cantankerous comments. He sounds so disloyal. Unlike other Monday Niters like Dr. Allen, Paul Booker, Carl Clark, Bubba Cleaver, Lloyd Greenfield, Ollie Hubbard and Ed West, Ike just blatantly “tells it like it is”. And readers everywhere just are appalled at his non-support of the home team.

But another old saying may apply. “It is what it is”. The Chiefs are shaky some days. But some days, they show great promise, especially among players like Derrick Johnson and Jamaal Charles and even Tamba Hali. Another old saying is that “you can’t make a silk purse out of a pig’s ear”. That means you have to accept what the Chiefs are and they are a good team…but not a great team like New England or Denver or New Orleans or even Seattle. Ike would say the Chiefs will not make it through the first round in the playoffs. But they made it this far and they will be trying to advance. And they will be on tv, when most teams will be watching.

Getting back to tv, we started out talking about Flip Wilson having his own tv show, way back when. And like all of our favorite shows, something is said that sticks with us all of our life. And it just may well wrap up this conversation about the Chiefs. Old Flip used to say somewhere during every telecast. What You See Is What You Get”

And that my friends describes our Kansas City Chiefs. So Ike needs to call on Flip to help him state his disdain, more appropriately.

He hopes Obama will win but was quick to remind everyone that elections can be stolen and referred us back to the Gore-Bush crap in Florida. . . Chatter, Conversation and Commentary

Super Bowl 42,

No Competition

February is Black History Month. It’s also the month when 22 states will decide its presidential preference. Of lesser importance, it is also the birthday month of our nation’s founding president, George Washington. And for lovers, the month houses Valentine’s Day. But all this will not hold a candle to Super Bowl 42, featuring the undefeated-going-perfect New England Patriots against the never-quitting New York Giants.

But, the game is only a small part. People will be talking about the million-dollar commercials. Already, one sponsor has set aside 16 million to be first in the commercial queue. Add to that the multi-million dollar half-time show (people are still talking about the Janet Jackson fiasco). And most of all, folks are lining the pockets of the Las Vegas casinos. . . rying to pick a for-sure winner. Right now, New England has the edge and everybody is talking about the Brady to Toomer nexus. But what about Little, less-famous brother Eli Manning.

Now, that is the real story of this Super Bowl. For nearly ten years, everybody has been talking about Big Brother, Priceless Peyton. And for the record, he was supposed to lead the Colts into Phoenix. Instead, it was Eli who is going to the Big Ball. There is hope for us all. Which brings me to my real message about the Monday Nite Footballers Group.

As we all elderize, we can go back to Super Bowl One and Four and any other. We saw Doug Williams set a Super Bowl record for most touchdowns scored in a Super Bowl. We’ve even witnessed running backs like Terrell Davis gallop from one end of the stadium to the other. We’ve known Kansas Citians, like Ralph Brown, who always went to the Super Bowl.

And finally, we remembered way back when the Chiefs were in the Super Bowl. I believe it was against Minnesota. I believe it was Otis Taylor who starred that day. He made a little kick going into the end zone.

Pardon us for not being sure about all the details. . . for it has been 39 years since the Chiefs were there. Hint. Hint. Do you hear us, Peterson and Edwards?

Spt.- Mon Nite Footballers

The Chiefs suck.

End of conversation.

Fortunately, the Monday Niters talk about more than the vicissitudes of this so-called professional football team. Out at Dr. Allen’s house, amid the Greek-influenced, romaine-based salad and another Greek concoction, man we talked about old remedies, used by our folks in the thirties and forties. And we focused our attention on some special senior citizens, those that are permanently imprinted in our memories.

Congressman Cleaver called from Washington to share some news about the over-demanded/under-supplied inaugural tickets. So, instead of beating some “dead Chiefs”, let’s look in on a not-so-typical Monday Niter word-fest.

DR. CHARLES ALLEN: Charles is sending Mrs. Mila Banks a special, belated Birthday wish. She believes the Chiefs will eventually develop into a fine team. That’s just like Charles, going out and recruiting people who still think the Chiefs can win. Mila says that the Monday Niters are “. . .men of little faith”. Right you are! But Charles isn’t. . . CHIEFS BY; 3

PAUL BOOKER: Booker is usually a nice guy, except when it comes to the Chiefs. Then, he turns into this “killer monster”, thinking that the Chiefs have no offense at all. He did smile when he acknowledged the wisdom and faith of his 84-year-old sister from Hayti, Missouri. This lady bore and reared thirteen children, in her lifetime. Booker says his mom used the yellow of an egg to reduce the swelling, caused by Mumps. Okkkkkkk! Anyway, BUFFALO BY 13

CARL CLARK: Carl claimed that his folks cured him of pneumonia by using skunk oil and honey. That one doesn’t pass the smell test. Carl mentioned seeing Mila Banks around town, especially at social events like Ivanhoe and Twin City functions. “She always talks about the Chiefs”, said Carl smiling. Smile or no smile. Smell or no smell. The Chiefs will be stampeded Sunday. . .BUFFALO BY 14

BUBBA CLEAVER: Cleaver is stuck in Washington, fighting for the bailout of the automakers. He says he cannot vote any other way because 2,000 or more jobs will be lost here in Kansas City, if he doesn’t support the bailout. Cleaver called the Monday Niters and told them he is being besieged with inaugural ticket requests. He is planning a lottery to address the thousands of requests...In the meantime, he thinks the Chiefs still have not found a way to win. . . BUFFALO BY 14.

IKE GARDNER: Ike believes the Chiefs are going to get a steady diet of Buffalo chips, Sunday. Ike has lost friends because of his no-patience position with the Chiefs. But he did smile when he spoke of Mila Banks. He remembers her as a great teacher at R.T. Coles. Ike even congratulated Mila for being such a fearless disciplinarian, something sorely needed in today’s schools. During the discussion about home remedies, Ike came up with something called “juniper tar and sugar” for colds. Will this work for the Chiefs? Apparently not. . . BUFFALO BY 13

LLOYD GREENFIELD: Lloyd said there was something called “666” for colds. Is this some kind of devil reference? And Lloyd chimed in about Mila Banks being at a recent Ivanhoe party. Lloyd reminded Mila that he was one of her late husband’s “jelly beans” (a term used frequently for students by Isaiah Banks). Speaking of jelly beans, Lloyd thinks that the Chiefs can’t even beat a piece of candy. BUFFALO BY 10

OLLIE HUBBARD Ollie talks with Mila Banks often, to check on her. Ollie said Mila and her friends went down to visit Carl Connor in Columbia recently. Growing up on 27th and Olive, Ollie says his mother gave him turpentine and sugar for colds. If the cold got real bad, she would apply “goose grease” all over his huge body. But, nothing can help these Chiefs with a win. Look for number 10 loss, Sunday. . . BUFFALO BY 20

JIM NUNNELLY: He brought up Carl Connor, who now resides in Columbia, Mo., near his nephew, Charles Allen. Connor reads this column religiously, even though he is at least ninety five. He is alert and like Mila, taught here in the Kansas City, Mo., School District for years. Carl is and has always been a Missouri Tiger sports fan. That’s good news because the Chiefs sure ain’t doing much. In Columbia, the old folks thought Vick’s saffe would cure colds, pneumonia, hangovers. . .everything. But, it can’t help the Chiefs BUFFALO BY 30

ED WEST: Ed is from rural Kansas and like all rural families, they relied on certain remedies for survival. His family consumed a lot of iron supplement, called 3-S Tonic. It must have helped because Ed is a picture of health and still uses “Three S” everyday. It seems that the Chiefs are taking something these days for they are definitely improving. But, according to Ed, not enough. BUFFALO BY 10.

Spt Monday niters

Monday Night Football

For the most part, we again ignored the awful plight of the Kansas City Chiefs, this past Monday. We were entertained at Ike’s house, out in Raytown. Ike claims he fixed the red beans and rice, laced with gumbo-like polish sausages and topped with onions. His family came to his rescue, with Lura, Shelley and Roosevelt providing various levels of hospitality. And the Chiefs, on a bye week, did not provide much conversational opportunities. Thus, the conversation drifted into lots of words about Lincoln University’s Homecoming weekend. Being alumni, several of our members attended the long-weekend and were greeted with the Temptations and a great half-time show, at the football game. Of particular note was the maturing Lincoln University Marching Band. Ollie Gates, favorite of old’ L.U., made his annual trek to Jefferson City, parked his motor-home on the football field, and provided Lincoln’s President with his annual donation.

But, Lincoln has changed dramatically, since the days of segregation. In fact, Lincoln is quite diverse, with many drive-in, day students having enrolled since the early seventies. That’s why it is referred to as an “historically black college and university”. So while the Chiefs will get slaughtered at Arrowhead Sunday, much of the conversation, for us, concentrated on an important topic, like Lincoln University. Unlike the Chiefs, Lincoln beat Xavier 56-7.

DR. CHARLES ALLEN Proud alumni of Lincoln...attended the homecoming ceremonies last week and immediately noticed a major difference, compared to the sixties...there were curfews then, especially for girls...there were no co-ed dorms.. this year, the Homecoming queen, a beautiful black lady, was escorted by a Caucasian guy.....and Charles clearly missed the “foot”, a collection of joints at the bottom of the famous hills...he missed Miss Leona’s famous chili-skillet-stew...she made sure that no Lincoln student ever went hungry....no coming back y’all...but Charles believes that the Chiefs are saying that “a setback is a set-up for a come-back” and predicts CHIEFS BY 7

PAUL BOOKER Also an alumni from the Bootheel...also misses Miss Leona’s cooking that combined everything into a single dish....Booker ate there twice a day because he hated the campus food...looks forward to meeting all of his old friends at Edna Brannock’s mother’s house each year...liked the Temptations. but coming back from Jefferson City, thought about the Chiefs and just knew that the Titans will Tennessee-waltz the Chiefs...TENNESSEE BY 13.

CARL CLARK Carl was rather quiet, this past Monday...in fact, he fell into a deep sleep a couple of times....usually talkative but rather subdued....he enjoyed attending Lincoln’s homecoming during his high school days...he didn’t go back after a girl threw some chitterlings on Apple Jack...he hasn’t been back since. someone must have thrown some chitlins on the Chiefs....Carl predicts TENNESSEE BY 14.

IKE GARDNER Ike’s affiliation with Lincoln arose because he was refused admission to Missouri University and they, then, suggested he enrolled at Lincoln, instead....noted that most black colleges, including Howard where he attended, had curfews based on the day of the week, allowing later times as the week progressed...did not ever attend Lincoln but has been a staunch supporter of HBCU’s...turning to the Chiefs, Ike is not impressed with Chiefs at all...thinks they can’t stop rolling downhill...TENNESSEE BY 25.

LLOYD GREENFIELD His cousin, Charlene Boswell attended Lincoln university....she was instrumental in organizing the Metropolitan Spiritual church...Lloyd was a sprinter at Lincoln High School way back in the early fifties....is producing a cd, featuring singers Jackie Mackrel and Geneva Price....keeps asking the question...have you ever seen a Tennessean walk....well, TENNESSEE BY 14.

OLLIE HUBBARD Graduated from Lincoln University in 1966....a major contributor to Lincoln....impressed with the growth of Lincoln over the years...attended “every” homecoming between 1967 and 2005, stopping because of a leg problems...salutes all the Kansas Citians who have attended Lincoln, including Donna Stewart, Ollie Gates, Georgia Buchanan, Bob Wheeler, and Gurnie Gunther...while he still plans to support Lincoln university, he has given up on the Chiefs entirely...TENNESSEE BY 30.

JIM NUNNELLY He chose to enroll at Missouri University, even though he had a full scholarship to Lincoln....attended several homecomings...as a high school student, won the Armistead Pride Journalism award three years in a row...lived in Columbia and knew many Lincoln alumni, including Erma Monroe, Juanita Dixon, Mary Patton, Joanne Herndon, Emma Turner, George Brooks, Montell Washington....lot of years have left by but not his faith in the Chiefs...CHIEFS BY 3

ED WEST Believes the Chiefs’ losing streak is about to take off...was a track and football athlete at Pittsburg State...competed in the long jump against Herman Gant...who was a miler at Lincoln.....Gant is still in great shape and walked every day from the Capitol hotel to the Lincoln campus during Lincoln’s Homecoming...like the rest of us, Ed is just plain tired of the Chiefs....TENNESSEE BY 30.

ROOSEVELT SCULARK Ike’s son-in-law....alumni of Jackson State and Howard, both hbcu’s...interacted with the Monday Niters throughout the evening...clean-cut and credible...believes the Chiefs’ secondary cannot stop anything or anybody...TENNESSEE BY 24...

Denver by 20.

SPT - MONDAY NITERS

First Lady Mila Lewis Banks,

Unchanging Things And The Chiefs

Mila Banks threw her own 100th birthday party, over at the Marriott at 45th and Main last Sunday. About 400 former students, fellow educators, and Christian friends stood in line to congratulate such a remarkable woman.

And at the dinner party, we, Monday Niters and wives attended and, were represented by cantankerous Ike Gardner. It was Ike’s idea to make “Ms. Banks” an honorary Monday Niter. That’s because she is a loyal reader and a good friend to so many of us.

So, Ike presented her with a trophy and told that crowd that she will be making her Chiefs’ predictions, right along side ours.

But last Monday night Gen Greenfield prepared a dinner of fresh sweet potatoes and freshly fried fish and a phenomenal salad. Who could argue about anything, with a meal like that in front of you?

So the Monday Niters, along with the Go-Along group spent the entire night, talking about how African Americans have been failed by various educational ploys. Here is how some of the conversation went. After that, we will give our predictions on the Buffalo-Chiefs game, coming up Sunday in Buffalo. Ms. Banks will make her prediction, as well. Here goes:

There was a time in Kansas City’s history when students coming from the Deep South were routinely put back a grade.

Also, during segregation days, black children from Independence, Excelsior Springs and Pleasant Hill were bussed into the Kansas City Mo., School District. Well into the sixties, there were still one-room schools in the state of Georgia.

In the Bootheel of Southeast Missouri, black students were bussed up to 60 miles each day to attend school. Because cotton was “king”, black kids went to school in “split sessions” i.e. their school year was interrupted to allow for the picking and chopping of cotton.

Ms. Banks attended school in Mount Vernon, about 46 miles west of Springfield, Mo. Because of its erudite faculty, Lincoln High school was rated highly nationwide.

Earning college credit while in high school is not a new thing. This was done in the sixties through such great educators like Jeremiah Cameron, Neal Herrford and Chester Anderson.

Dr. Charles Allen attended Sumner Elementary and Ike, Carl and Lloyd attended, Wendell Phillips way back in the thirties and forties. Ed West attended school in Osawatomie, Kansas and Jim Nunnelly attended segregated schools in Columbia, Mo.

Ms. Banks received unexpected help with her tuition to Lincoln university, way back in the thirties.

Today, educating black kids is still an issue in Kansas City.Students who want to leave the unaccredited Kansas City, Mo., School District to attend a nearby accredited school district, are met with the problem of who is to pay for all of this.....the more things change, the more they stay the same.

Not so for the Chiefs....they will be 9-0 at the end of the day, Sunday. Here are our thoughts about the Chiefs:

Monday Niters

Marvin Gaye, VIPs, And The Chiefs

Marvin Gaye is best known for his smash hit song, “Let’s Get It On”. But my favorite, Marvin Gaye song is “Inner City Blues”. In this seventies song, Marvin highlights what it’s like to be an outsider and a non-participant in the American dream. In fact, at one point in the song he screams. . . “makes me want to holler and throw up both my hands”.

Well, given the last few seasons, the Chiefs have sure made a lot of fans “holler and throw up both hands. But, there is a new sheriff and deputies in town and let’s just wait and see what if we can lower our collective hands. Okay, it was important for me to say that first.

Speaking of importance, the Monday Niters were special guests of the Kansas City Chiefs this past Tuesday. Yes, the Chiefs treated us like we were royalty up at their training camp in St. Joseph. They gave us special parking passes, covered and cool sideline seating, lots of beverages, and a ton of material. . . all to emphasize that the 2012 Chiefs are no longer alive. They are dead. And in their place are notables like a new quarterback, Alex Smith and a first-picked, first-round offensive lineman named Eric Fisher. The new coach, Andy Reid, walked around the field like he had “star-trek” hope for these new, improved Chiefs. Joining from the past are the exciting Jamaal Charles, at running back and Dwayne Bowe, acrobatic receiver. This mix of old and new will get its first opportunity-to-impress tonight, against the New Orleans Saints, in a doesn’t-count, preseason game.

But it does count for the Chiefs. If the Chiefs should lose, the fans will “holler” louder than before. And the renewed promise of a “new day” will slipper into the usual “hang-ups, let-downs, bad breaks, and set-backs”. And of course, there will be a designated player, appointed to tell the fans why we should believe that the Chiefs still deserve our support and that they “will” be better than last year’s 2-14 record. But, oh if they win!

The “holler” will be converted into “screaming for joy”. The hang-ups will be reduced. The let-downs will be lifted up. The bad breaks will be forgiven. And the set-backs will be forgotten. And we will look forward to the regular season, with “red colors” draped all over us.

But, like a line in Marvin Gaye’s song says, “only God knows where the Chiefs are headed”.

We sure don’t.

Monday Niters who were VIPs at the Chiefs’ training camp, August 6 included Dr. Charles Allen, Paul Booker, Carl Clark, Ike Gardner, Lloyd Greenfield, Jim Nunnelly and Ed West. Also on the guest list were Robert Allen, Clancy Parker, Joe Webster, Sanders Martin, and Ron Walton.
